response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile senior engineer with strong distributed-systems and low-latency experience, but their C++ exposure is minimal and dated — roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor thesis — while the last decade has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and full-stack work. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, industrial IoT protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), and IEC 62443 secure development practice, none of which appear on this resume; C++ is not even listed in the skills section.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Event-driven architecture and microservices background, including an open-source geo-data platform on Kubernetes
Strong mentoring, testing culture (TDD, workshops, courses) and technical communication track record
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months) and over a decade old; skills list omits C++ entirely
No industrial automation, MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus, or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-engineering exposure
No Azure experience and career trajectory points toward web/full-stack rather than embedded/industrial C++

key_factors.factor: No industrial IoT or IEC 62443 background
Strong distributed and low-latency systems experience
